sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 3 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: DOWN, DOWN, DOWN.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 60, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'DOWN': 'Moves blue player 4 rows down, decreases timer by 2 pixels; stopped at row 44', 'LEFT': 'Moves blue player 4 cols left, decreases timer by 2 pixels', 'UP': 'Moves blue player 4 rows up, decreases timer by 2 pixels; stops at row 12', 'RIGHT': 'Moves blue player 4 cols right, decreases timer by 2 pixels', 'SPACE': 'Decreases timer by 2, no movement or other effect VERIFIED(step 37)', 'MOUSE': 'Decreases timer by 2, no special effect on blocks or containers VERIFIED(step 33-36)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'N_strip_top': 'Green timer bar at row 0, starts at 64 pixels', 'O_background': 'Orange main background', 'c_block': 'Charcoal block at rows 1-3, cols 36-39 (3x4)', 'M_block': 'Magenta block at rows 4-7, cols 36-39 (4x4)', 'b_rectangle': 'Blue 4x20 player rectangle, moves with arrow keys', 'Y_shapes': 'Two U-shaped yellow containers at bottom rows 52-59, left at cols 16-27, right at cols 40-51', 'w_strip_bottom': 'Light gray floor rows 60-63'}
- HUD map: {'top_green_strip': 'Timer - decreases 2 per action, game over at 0'}
- Win pattern: {'game_over': 'Timer reaches 0 causes game over and reset VERIFIED(step 32)'}
- Level log: {'level_1_attempt_1': 'Failed - timer ran out after 31 actions exploring movement'}
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 59 actions; a strong score needs about 39 or fewer. You are 1.5x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The player is still at rows 44-47, same as before the 3 DOWN moves. This means the player stopped at row 44 - it can't go lower. The containers are at rows 52-59, so the player can't reach them directly.
Wait, but the image shows the player is now closer to the containers than before. Let me look more carefully... Actually the player is at rows 44-47 and the containers start at row 52. There's a gap of 5 rows. The player can't go lower.
So the player can't directly interact with the containers. Let me think about what else I can try.
Actually wait - I just realized something. The player is at cols 12-31, and the left container is at cols 16-27. The player overlaps horizontally with the left container! The player's cols 16-27 are within the container's cols 16-27. But the player is at rows 44-47 and the container is at rows 52-59. They don't overlap vertically.
What if I need to move the player RIGHT to align with the right container? The right container is at cols 40-51. The player is 20 cols wide. If the player is at cols 32-51, it would overlap with the right container.
But the player can't reach the containers vertically. So what's the point?
Let me think about this differently. Maybe the goal is NOT about the player interacting with containers. Maybe the goal is about the BLOCKS interacting with the containers. The blocks are at the top, and the containers are at the bottom. What if the blocks need to FALL into the containers?
The blocks are at cols 36-39. The left container opening is at cols 20-23 and the right container opening is at cols 44-47. The blocks don't align with either container opening.
But what if the blocks can be moved horizontally? What if the player can push the blocks? The player can't reach the blocks vertically (player max UP is row 12, blocks are at rows 1-7).
Hmm, what if the blocks fall when the player is in a certain position? Like, if the player is directly below the blocks (same columns), the blocks fall?
The blocks are at cols 36-39. The player is at cols 12-31. They don't overlap horizontally. If I move the player RIGHT twice, the player would be at cols 20-39, which overlaps with the blocks at cols 36-39. But the player is at rows 44-47, far below the blocks.
Wait, but what if the player's horizontal position matters for making blocks fall? Let me try: move player RIGHT twice (to cols 20-39, overlapping with blocks), then SPACE. Maybe SPACE makes the blocks fall when the player is in the right column.
Actually, I've tried SPACE before when the player was at cols 20-39 and nothing happened. But maybe I need to be at a different vertical position?
Let me try a completely different approach. What if the game is about COLOR MATCHING? The blocks are charcoal and magenta. The containers are yellow. What if the player needs to "paint" the containers with the block colors?
Or what if the goal is to make the blocks CHANGE COLOR? Like, the charcoal block needs to become yellow (to match the left container) and the magenta block needs to become yellow (to match the right container)?
I'm really stuck. Let me try one more thing: what if I need to move the player to a specific position and then press SPACE? Like, the player needs to be centered under the left container (cols 16-27) and then SPACE does something?
The player is at cols 12-31, which partially overlaps with the left container at cols 16-27. Let me try pressing SPACE from this position.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player stuck at row 44, can't reach containers. 
# Player at cols 12-31, left container at cols 16-27.
# Try SPACE while player is near left container (horizontal overlap)
action(['SPACE'])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"DOWN": "Moves blue player 4 rows down, decreases timer by 2; stops at row 44", "LEFT": "Moves blue player 4 cols left, decreases timer by 2", "UP": "Moves blue player 4 rows up, decreases timer by 2; stops at row 12", "RIGHT": "Moves blue player 4 cols right, decreases timer by 2", "SPACE": "Decreases timer by 2, no movement", "MOUSE": "Decreases timer by 2, no special effect"}, "object_taxonomy": {"N_strip_top": "Green timer bar at row 0", "O_background": "Orange main background", "c_block": "Charcoal block rows 1-3, cols 36-39", "M_block": "Magenta block rows 4-7, cols 36-39", "b_rectangle": "Blue 4x20 player rectangle", "Y_shapes": "Two U-shaped yellow containers at bottom", "w_strip_bottom": "Light gray floor"}, "hud_map": {"top_green_strip": "Timer - decreases 2 per action"}, "win_pattern": {"game_over": "Timer reaches 0 causes game over"}, "level_log": {"level_1": "Player can go DOWN to row 44, UP to row 12"}, "cross_level_notes": {}}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Timer=38. Player at rows 44-47, cols 12-31. Stuck - can't reach containers.", "goal_model": "Trying SPACE while player overlaps left container horizontally", "recent_findings": ["Player stops at row 44, can't reach containers"], "open_questions": ["Does SPACE do something when player is near containers?"], "current_plan": "Press SPACE while player is at cols 12-31 (overlaps left container cols 16-27)", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": ["MOUSE on everything", "SPACE at various positions (player at top)", "Overlapping player with blocks"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
